content:
* We acknowledge that we are gathered on the traditional and unceded territory of the Algonquin Anishinaabe Nation. For generations, they have been the stewards of this land, and their deep connection to it continues to this day. We recognize the enduring presence and contributions of the Algonquin people to this region. We also acknowledge the painful history of colonization and the displacement of Indigenous communities from their ancestral lands. We commit ourselves to learning, understanding, and honoring the Algonquin Nation's history and culture. We recognize the importance of working collaboratively to create a more just and equitable future for all who call this land home.
* Nous reconnaissons que nous sommes réunis sur le territoire traditionnel et non cédé de la nation algonquine anishinaabe. Depuis des générations, ils sont les gardiens de cette terre et le lien profond qui les unit à elle perdure encore aujourd'hui. Nous reconnaissons la présence et les contributions durables du peuple algonquin dans cette région. Nous reconnaissons également l'histoire douloureuse de la colonisation et du déplacement des communautés autochtones de leurs terres ancestrales. Nous nous engageons à apprendre, à comprendre et à honorer l'histoire et la culture de la nation algonquine. Nous reconnaissons l'importance de travailler en collaboration pour créer un avenir plus juste et plus équitable pour tous ceux qui vivent sur cette terre.
________________
sentences: * We acknowledge that we are gathered on the traditional and unceded territory of the Algonquin Anishinaabe Nation.
For generations, they have been the stewards of this land, and their deep connection to it continues to this day.
We recognize the enduring presence and contributions of the Algonquin people to this region.
We also acknowledge the painful history of colonization and the displacement of Indigenous communities from their ancestral lands.
We commit ourselves to learning, understanding, and honoring the Algonquin Nation's history and culture.
We recognize the importance of working collaboratively to create a more just and equitable future for all who call this land home.
* Nous reconnaissons que nous sommes réunis sur le territoire traditionnel et non cédé de la nation algonquine anishinaabe.
Depuis des générations, ils sont les gardiens de cette terre et le lien profond qui les unit à elle perdure encore aujourd'hui.
Nous reconnaissons la présence et les contributions durables du peuple algonquin dans cette région.
Nous reconnaissons également l'histoire douloureuse de la colonisation et du déplacement des communautés autochtones de leurs terres ancestrales.
Nous nous engageons à apprendre, à comprendre et à honorer l'histoire et la culture de la nation algonquine.

Nous reconnaissons l'importance de travailler en collaboration pour créer un avenir plus juste et plus équitable pour tous ceux qui vivent sur cette terre.
________________

content:
* 73 kit sales have been completed - raised over $9980
* If you require a locker for the school year, please see the executive perks page on our internal website
* Motioned to increase first aid training / decrease smart serve training
* Motioned to move budget line around for 101er/guide shirts, bandanas, beer steins and shot glasses
* Motioned to increase patch budget for 101 week from 1000 to 1300 - paid and looking to receive early September
* MrT happened, went over audit training, and summer levies will arrive within the next 2 weeks

content:
* Attended PhilRTs
* Reached out to CSSA and SSA to coordinate shinerama garage sale → will be August 25
* We can either do a joint donation w/ them or keep our proceeds for our final 101 week shine donation
* Also they mentioned we can encourage guides to sell their stuff too if we want lol
* Met with cat rescue volunteer coordinator → events are approved including cat cafe as long as they come to scout location
* Some new events were suggested by her, including a design competition lol, but will be free
* Chose eng ball charity and reached out for letter of recognition, starting negotiations for venues asap
* Nimet (charity committee sponsorship) was able to get Tims and Bridgehead product donations for shine breakfast!!🎉

content:
* Hayley: uOEC theme? Comms are a bit of a mess
Daniel: Hasn’t been discussed much, will be brought up at a meeting
Hayley: Asked for clarification from Reema but there isn’t much information readily available.
Daniel: Will talk about this tomorrow (meeting). Not the priority right now with signups and the post. Will have it prepared towards 101 Week, promo request to be sent after 101 Week.
Hayley: Perfect, thank you.

content:
* Daniel: It is allowed to have someone that is not in engineering / represented programs participate in OEC so long they are an associate member. CEC is a different process and they would have to be opted in on a July 1st deadline. For OEC, should CS students be allowed?
* Hayley: Don’t promote, but we should try to allow it and ask CSSA to pay their fees.
* Daniel: In the event that they won, the team could be invalid if some of the members are not granted associate membership. We should confirm that CSSA will pay and that they have Associate Membership before uOEC actually happens.
* Eric: The student list last year was not referenced.
* Hayley: Easier to say no comp-sci students. Isn’t that how it’s supposed to be?
* Daniel: Yeah, they technically shouldn't be going, It's easier to say no, rather than exploit the workaround of granting them associate membership. We just need to make sure we verify student lists before the competition and clearly state signup requirements.
* Daniel: We need to opt multidisciplinary design into being CFES members so that they are eligible to attend CEC. We sent an email to the CFES VPFA, but missed the July 1st deadline.
* Ashna: I'm still waiting on CFES VPFA to get back to me.

* Hayley: Don’t promote, but we should try to allow it and ask CSSA to pay their fees.

We should confirm that CSSA will pay and that they have Associate Membership before uOEC actually happens.
Eric: The student list last year was not referenced.

Isn’t that how it’s supposed to be?
Daniel:
Yeah, they technically shouldn't be going, It's easier to say no, rather than exploit the workaround of granting them associate membership.
We just need to make sure we verify student lists before the competition and clearly state signup requirements.
Daniel: We need to opt multidisciplinary design into being CFES members so that they are eligible to attend CEC.
We sent an email to the CFES VPFA, but missed the July 1st deadline.
* Ashna: I'm still waiting on CFES VPFA to get back to me.
